response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong software engineering experience across multiple languages and domains, including geospatial systems (GISCollective) and low-latency applications (Dunnhumby). However, his primary expertise lies in scripting languages (TypeScript, Node.js, Python-like languages) and D/Rust, with limited recent experience in C++—the core requirement for this role. While his geospatial project (GISCollective) is relevant, it lacks the routing/navigation focus and distributed systems depth specified in the job description. His adaptability is a plus, but the gap in C++ and routing-specific experience warrants a cautious 'maybe' recommendation.
strengths: Strong geospatial systems experience (GISCollective) with Kubernetes deployment and event-driven architecture
Proven ability to optimize performance in low-latency applications (Dunnhumby, National Instruments)
Demonstrated adaptability across multiple languages and tech stacks (TypeScript, C++, D, Rust)
Open-source contributions with testable, maintainable code (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
concerns: Limited recent C++ experience (last significant C++ work at National Instruments in 2015)
No explicit experience with routing/navigation systems or traffic integration pipelines
Geospatial experience (GISCollective) focuses on data collection rather than routing tile optimization
Primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js) diverges from job requirements (C++, Rust, Linux server apps)
